2604.11201.title: CocoaBench: Evaluating Unified Digital Agents in the Wild
2604.11201.paper_summary: LLM agents now perform strongly in software engineering, deep research, GUI automation, and various other applications, while recent agent scaffolds and models are increasingly integrating these capabilities into unified systems. Yet, most evaluations still test these capabilities in isolation, which leaves a gap for more diverse use cases that require agents to combine different capabilities. We introduce CocoaBench, a benchmark for unified digital agents built from human-designed, long-horizon tasks that require flexible composition of vision, search, and coding. Tasks are specified only by an instruction and an automatic evaluation function over the final output, enabling reliable and scalable evaluation across diverse agent infrastructures. We also present CocoaAgent, a lightweight shared scaffold for controlled comparison across model backbones. Experiments show that current agents remain far from reliable on CocoaBench, with the best evaluated system achieving only 45.1% success rate. Our analysis further points to substantial room for improvement in reasoning and planning, tool use and execution, and visual grounding.
2604.11201.paper_summary_zh: LLM代理現在在軟體工程、深度研究、GUI自動化以及各種其他應用中表現出色，而最近的代理架構和模型正日益將這些能力整合到統一系統中。<lb/>然而，大多數評估仍然在孤立的情況下測試這些能力，這使得需要代理結合不同能力的更多多樣化用例存在空白。<lb/>我們介紹了CocoaBench，一個基於人類設計的、需要靈活組合視覺、搜索和編碼的長期任務的統一數位代理基準。<lb/>任務僅通過指令和對最終輸出進行自動評估的函數來指定，從而實現了在多樣化代理基礎設施中可靠且可擴展的評估。<lb/>我們還介紹了CocoaAgent，一個輕量級的共享架構，用於在模型骨幹之間進行受控比較。<lb/>實驗顯示，目前的代理在CocoaBench上的可靠性仍然遠未達到，最佳評估系統的成功率僅為45.1%。<lb/>我們的分析進一步指出，在推理和規劃、工具使用和執行以及視覺基礎方面仍有相當大的改進空間。
2604.11201.author: CocoaBench Team et.al.

Xing, Zhengzhong Liu, Rupesh Kumar Srivastava, Zhiting Hu
